factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the insurance and financial services segments.

evidence.text: Revenues were $2,145.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2017 compared to $1,995.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2016, an increase of $150.0 million or 7.5%. Excluding revenues of $61.3 million from recent acquisitions, our revenue growth was $88.7 million or 4.5%. Revenues within our Decision Analytics segment increased by $54.7 million or 4.3%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments, indicating positive momentum.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is present but not the primary driver of total revenue growth.

evidence.text: Excluding revenues of $61.3 million from recent acquisitions, our revenue growth was $88.7 million or 4.5%. Revenues within our Decision Analytics segment, excluding recent acquisitions, increased by $54.7 million or 4.3%. Organic growth is evident but acquisitions contributed significantly to total revenue growth.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is present but not the primary driver, with mixed signals from evidence.

factors.summary: The insurance and financial services segments are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Our insurance revenue increased $84.9 million or 12.1%. Excluding revenues of $17.1 million from recent acquisitions, our insurance revenue increased $67.8 million or 9.7%. The financial services revenue increased $17.2 million or 13.4%. Excluding revenues of $20.8 million from recent acquisitions, our financial services revenue decreased $3.6 million or 2.8%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Insurance and financial services segments are growing fastest, showing positive market momentum.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to higher expenses.

evidence.text: Our recent acquisitions accounted for an increase of $45.3 million in cost of revenues, primarily related to salaries and employee benefits. Excluding the impact of our recent acquisitions, our cost of revenues increased $24.1 million or 3.4%. The increase was primarily due to increases in salaries and employee benefits cost of $30.7 million, data costs and data processing fees of $6.1 million, information technology expense of $1.7 million and other operating costs of $0.1 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to higher expenses, which is a negative trend.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to tax reform and cost management.

evidence.text: The effective tax rate was 19.7% for the year ended December 31, 2017 compared to 30.9% for the year ended December 31, 2016. The decrease in the effective tax rate in 2017 compared to 2016 was primarily due to lowered federal income tax rates as a result of U.S. Tax Reform and the adoption of ASU No. 2016-09, partially offset by legislation enacted in the U.K.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are improving significantly due to tax reform and cost management.

factors.summary: The company is actively pursuing acquisitions to expand its capabilities.

evidence.text: We acquired twenty-one businesses since January 1, 2015. These acquisitions affect the comparability of our consolidated results of operations between periods. See Note 8 to our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 10-K for further discussions on the below acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively pursuing acquisitions, indicating strategic growth activity.
